subject: Re: particles.star file not created for new species
body:
Hi Juha,

Why do you have an --ignore_unmatched there? Could that be the culprit?

Cheers,

Alister

On Sun, 17 Nov 2024 at 01:29, Juha Huiskonen <juha.huiskonen@gmail.com>
wrote:

> I noticed that also ribosome_localres.star file is not created.  All other
> files are there.  So somehow my issue is related to writing the STAR files.
> It's peculiar because everything worked fine with the tutorial data.
>
> On Friday, 15 November 2024 at 23:51:21 UTC+2 Juha Huiskonen wrote:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> I am running the following command to create a new species
>>
>> MTools create_species \
>> --population m/g2.population \
>> --name ribosome \
>> --diameter 350 \
>> --sym C1 \
>> --temporal_samples 1 \
>> --half1 relion_ribosome/Refine3D/job007/run_half1_class001_unfil.mrc \
>> --half2 relion_ribosome/Refine3D/job007/run_half2_class001_unfil.mrc \
>> --mask m/mask_ribosome.mrc \
>> --particles_relion relion_ribosome/Refine3D/job007/run_data.star \
>> --angpix_resample 2.405 \
>> --lowpass 20 \
>> --ignore_unmatched
>>
>> However, when I checked the folder
>> m/species/ribosome_b91790ab/
>>
>> I noticed that ribosome_particles.star file had not been created. This
>> file is expected when I try to run M refinement.  Any suggestions why this
>> might be happening?
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Juha
>>
> --

> .
>
